Q1_atoms.verbatim_quote: But I think some people are trying. And it turns out that Mac minis, for example, are a very good fit for some of the LLMs because it's all if you're doing batch one inference, this is all super memory bound.

And this problem, this long context, really arises because we're trying to find the workarounds, which in turn is because the old rules of machine learning dictate that we can't change the model ways at test time. So, I mean, I guess the simple idea is, you know, what if we also do gradient descent on the model at test time?
